title: VHS Defects
description: The failure modes of a worn VHS tape, layered over moving footage (a synthwave scene, or drop an image to degrade your own). Processing is done in YIQ — the way analogue video separates luma from chroma — which is what makes it read as VHS: chroma bleed and lag from the tape's tiny colour bandwidth, per-scanline tracking jitter, the torn head-switching noise band along the bottom, bright dropout streaks where the oxide flaked off, tape snow, scanlines, and a vertical-hold roll that slips now and then. Tracking, chroma, snow, dropouts, scanlines, roll, and wear are live and audio-mappable (beats knock the tracking).
